references.name: The Andy Warhol Museum
references.takeaway: Use a strict 4-step type scale on a neutral black-and-white scaffold, and let full-bleed artwork or hero imagery provide all the color

applied.implementation: Four-step Bebas Neue type scale: stat numbers (3.2rem), view titles (2.6rem), panel titles (1.2rem), labels (0.85rem). The interface scaffold is entirely black/white/grey, with color coming from content panels and accent fills.
Stat panels use bold halftone-patterned colored backgrounds (pink, cyan, green, yellow) while the surrounding layout stays monochrome white with black borders - content provides the chromatic energy.
Project cover cards have full-bleed colored hero sections (140px tall) that act as the visual focal point, while the card info area below remains on white.

references.name: Roy Lichtenstein Foundation
references.takeaway: Apply a visible halftone dot-pattern texture to background fills and image overlays, and restrict the palette to flat primary colors with heavy black outlines on all container edges

applied.implementation: Radial-gradient halftone patterns applied to: ambient background (18px grid), stat panel overlays (10px grid), cover image overlays (8px grid), button hover states (6px grid), and whiteboard node fills (10px grid). All via CSS radial-gradient.

applied.element: Heavy black outlines on all containers
applied.implementation: Every card, panel, column, frame, and interactive element has 3-4px solid black (#0a0a0a) borders. No rounded corners (radius-hard: 0px) to maintain the hard comic-book print aesthetic.
Four flat colors (pink, cyan, green, yellow) used as solid fills for column headers, entity headers, section headers, avatar backgrounds, and tag badges - never gradients, always solid flat blocks.

references.name: MoMA Design Store
references.takeaway: Build a strict uniform-square product grid on pure white where every card is the same size and proportion, then use a single high-chroma accent for interactive badges and price callouts

applied.implementation: Projects view uses a strict 3-column grid where every magazine cover card has identical dimensions, uniform 140px hero image height, and consistent padding. The grid sits on pure white with identical proportions.

Interactive elements (tags, status badges, action buttons, activity badges) use high-chroma electric pink (#ff1493) as the primary interactive accent. Secondary actions use cyan. The accent colors are reserved exclusively for interactive and status-indicator elements.
Kanban cards all share identical structure: halftone color strip (6px), title, description, footer with tag and avatar. Uniform padding and border treatment creates gallery-wall consistency.
